source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of mesenchymal progenitor cells in adipose tissue
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal fibroblast/adipocyte progenitor cells
Fibrillin-1 is an extracellular matrix gene characteristic of adipocyte precursor cells
Microfibril-associated protein 5 marks stromal/fibroblastic adipose progenitors
CD248 (endosialin) is a marker of mesenchymal stromal/pericyte-like adipocyte precursors
IGFBP5 is highly expressed in adipose progenitors and regulates IGF signaling in adipogenesis
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a hallmark of mesenchymal/fibroblastic adipocyte precursors
SFRP4 is a Wnt signaling modulator enriched in adipose stromal progenitor populations
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and a canonical B cell marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a definitive B cell surface marker used in clinical identification
Immunoglobulin kappa constant region reflects active immunoglobulin production by B cells
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is a B cell survival receptor essential for B cell maturation
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR signaling
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed in B cells for MHC class II antigen presentation
J chain is critical for polymeric immunoglobulin assembly in antibody-secreting B cells
CD37 is a tetraspanin predominantly expressed on B cells involved in immune signaling
TRGC2 encodes the T cell receptor gamma constant region, defining gamma delta T cells
CD7 is a T/NK cell marker highly expressed on gamma delta T cells
LEF1 is a transcription factor associated with T cell development and naive/memory states
TCF7 is a key transcription factor for T cell identity and stemness
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor expressed on gamma delta T cell subsets
XCL1 is a chemokine characteristically produced by gamma delta T cells and NK cells
IL7R marks tissue-resident gamma delta T cells and supports their homeostasis
CD8A is the canonical surface marker defining CD8+ T cells
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule characteristic of cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic effector protease in CD8+ cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme H is a cytotoxic serine protease enriched in effector CD8+ T cells
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic granule-mediated killing by CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule membrane protein highly expressed in effector CD8+ T cells
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated cytotoxic effector lymphocytes
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
CD3G is a T cell receptor complex component confirming T cell lineage
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein and canonical endothelial cell marker
PECAM1 (CD31) is a definitive endothelial cell surface marker
Von Willebrand factor is a classic endothelial marker involved in hemostasis
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor specific to endothelial cells
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor important for vascular development
SOX17 is a transcription factor critical for endothelial cell identity and arterial specification
CAVIN2 is a caveolae-associated protein enriched in endothelial cells
PLVAP is an endothelial-specific protein forming fenestral diaphragms
Aquaporin 1 is highly expressed in endothelial cells for water transport
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor marking immature/developing NK cells
Granzyme K is enriched in immature/CD56bright NK cells compared to mature NK cells
Granulysin is a cytotoxic molecule expressed in NK cells
CD2 is an adhesion molecule expressed on NK cells important for activation
XCL2 is a chemokine characteristically produced by immature NK cells
SELL (CD62L) marks immature/CD56bright NK cells with lymph node homing capacity
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK cell receptor expressed during NK cell maturation
CRTAM is an activation marker on immature NK and T cells
IL1B is a hallmark pro-inflammatory cytokine of classically activated macrophages
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a key chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages for neutrophil recruitment
CXCL3 is an inflammatory chemokine highly expressed by activated macrophages
CCL20 is produced by inflammatory macrophages to recruit immune cells
CD163 is a scavenger receptor and macrophage lineage marker
SOD2 is upregulated in inflammatory macrophages as part of the oxidative stress response
PTGS2 (COX-2) is a key inflammatory enzyme in activated macrophages
NLRP3 inflammasome is a hallmark of inflammatory macrophage activation
OLR1 (LOX-1) is an oxidized LDL receptor upregulated in inflammatory macrophages
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical marker broadly expressed across ILC populations
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 and broadly marks ILCs
KIT (CD117) is expressed on ILC progenitors and multiple ILC subsets
IL7R (CD127) is a key marker of innate lymphoid cells required for their development
IL1RL1 (ST2/IL-33R) is a canonical ILC2 marker within the broader ILC population
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is highly expressed on ILCs, particularly ILC2s
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated ILCs
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILCs, particularly ILC2s
IL32 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine highly expressed in ILC1s
XCL1 is a chemokine produced by ILC1s and NK-like cells
IL7R (CD127) distinguishes ILC1s from NK cells within the group 1 ILC family
ETS1 is a transcription factor important for ILC1 development and function
ITGB1 (CD29) is expressed on tissue-resident ILC1s for tissue retention
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical ILC marker expressed on ILC1s
SPOCK2 is enriched in ILC1 populations in tissue-resident innate lymphoid cells
IL1RL1 (ST2) is the canonical ILC2 marker and IL-33 receptor
GATA3 is the master transcription factor defining ILC2 identity
RORA is a key transcription factor required for ILC2 development
HPGD (prostaglandin dehydrogenase) is a well-known ILC2 marker gene
KLRB1 (CD161) is highly expressed on ILC2s as a canonical ILC surface marker
IL7R (CD127) is essential for ILC2 survival and a defining surface marker
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILC2s
SLAMF1 marks ILC2s in human tissues
KIT (CD117) is a canonical surface marker of ILC3s
CCR6 defines the LTi-like ILC3 subset and is a key ILC3 marker
IL7R (CD127) is a defining marker of ILC3s

DLL1 (Notch ligand) is expressed by ILC3s and involved in their function
RUNX2 is a transcription factor associated with ILC3 differentiation
AQP3 is enriched in ILC3 populations in mucosal tissues
KIT (CD117) is a canonical marker of ILC precursors
IL7R (CD127) is essential for ILC precursor survival and development

TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on ILC precursors
SELL (CD62L) marks circulating ILC precursors with tissue homing capacity
GATA3 is expressed in ILC precursors as they commit to the ILC lineage
CD7 is expressed on early lymphoid progenitors including ILC precursors
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on ILC precursors

GPNMB is a glycoprotein highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages

CD68 is a canonical macrophage marker confirming macrophage lineage
LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase) is essential for lipid processing in lipid-associated macrophages

Granulysin is a major cytotoxic effector molecule in mature NK cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic protease in mature/CD56dim NK cells
FCGR3A (CD16) is the canonical marker of mature CD56dim NK cells mediating ADCC
KLRF1 (NKp80) is an NK cell-specific activating receptor

SPON2 is specifically expressed in mature cytotoxic NK cells

CD247 (CD3 zeta chain) is part of the NK cell signaling complex
MYOM2 is enriched in mature NK cells and distinguishes them from other lymphocytes
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical MAIT cell marker, with high expression defining this population
RORA is a transcription factor characteristic of MAIT cells
IL7R (CD127) is highly expressed on MAIT cells

TCR gamma chain expression reflects the semi-invariant TCR of MAIT cells
NCR3 (NKp30) is expressed on MAIT cells as an innate-like receptor
Granzyme K is a cytotoxic molecule enriched in MAIT cells
CD8A marks the predominant CD8+ MAIT cell subset
S100A9 is a canonical myeloid cell marker forming calprotectin with S100A8
S100A8 is a hallmark myeloid marker highly expressed in monocytes and neutrophils

AIF1 (IBA1) is a myeloid lineage marker expressed in monocytes/macrophages
TYROBP (DAP12) is a signaling adaptor essential for myeloid cell function

IL7R (CD127) is a canonical marker of naive CD4+ T cells

CD2 is a T cell surface marker expressed on CD4+ T cells

ITK is a tyrosine kinase important for TCR signaling in CD4+ T cells
TRBC2 encodes the TCR beta constant region confirming T cell identity
CD8A is the canonical marker of CD8+ T cells
CD8B is co-expressed with CD8A on conventional CD8+ T cells
Granzyme K is characteristic of early effector/naive CD8+ T cells

RUNX3 is a master transcription factor for CD8+ T cell lineage commitment
DUSP2 is a phosphatase involved in TCR signaling in CD8+ T cells
XCL1 is a chemokine highly produced by NK-like cells
XCL2 is a chemokine characteristically produced by NK-like lymphocytes
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory NK receptor on NK-like cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is a signaling adaptor for NK cell activating receptors
FCER1G is a signaling chain for NK cell receptors
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK cell receptor expressed on NK-like cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule protein in NK and NK-like cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is expressed on NK-like innate lymphocytes
IRF8 is a transcription factor involved in NK-like cell differentiation
S100A9 is a canonical neutrophil marker forming calprotectin

SERPINB2 (PAI-2) is upregulated in activated neutrophils

CD93 is expressed on neutrophils and involved in phagocytosis
SLC11A1 (NRAMP1) is a neutrophil phagosomal membrane protein
AQP9 is an aquaporin highly expressed in neutrophils
LST1 is highly expressed in nonclassical monocytes
MS4A7 is a canonical marker of nonclassical (CD16+) monocytes
LILRB2 is an inhibitory receptor enriched in nonclassical monocytes

HPGD is involved in prostaglandin metabolism and marks Tregs

CD163 is a monocyte/macrophage scavenger receptor
